sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.61 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: A subgroup$H$of a group$G$is fully invariant\/ if$(H) H$for every endomorphism$$of~$G$. Let$G$be a finite group such that$G$has a fully invariant subgroup of order$d$for every$d$dividing$|G|$. Must$G$be cyclic? { D.\,MacHale} No: take$G=C_p C_{p^2}$, where$C_p,C_{p^2}$are cyclic$p$-groups of orders$p,\;p^2$(A.\,Abdol\-lahi, Letter of 4 March 2009).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
